author: Jonathan Grynspan
message:
Implement the back end of `CommandLine.executablePath`. (#90476)

This PR implements the C++ back end of `CommandLine.executablePath` as
approved in
[SE-0513](https://github.com/swiftlang/swift-evolution/blob/main/proposals/0513-commandline-executablepath.md).

The front end that exposes a `FilePath`-based API is blocked on the
existence of `FilePath` in the standard library, but some toolchain
components like Swift Testing need to back-deploy and can't link to the
System package directly, so they need to directly use the C++
implementation.

Darwin doesn't need this implementation as it has
`_NSGetExecutablePath()` that will be used by the back-deployable Darwin
implementation instead of this helper function.

author: Allan Shortlidge
message:
Sema: Remove the availability_string_subscript_migration diagnostic.

This diagnostic was introduced to help migrate code from Swift 3, where
String's Range subscript returned String, to Swift 4, where it returns
Substring. The obsoleted-in-Swift-4 shims that kept the old subscripts around
were removed from the stdlib in 2018, so the diagnostic can no longer be
triggered by any real code. The test/Migrator tests that were meant to exercise
this fix-it were XFAIL'd from the day they were added and can be deleted as
well.

author: Doug Gregor
message:
[CMake] Consistently treat NO_FREESTANDING_CXX as an option

already declared `NO_FREESTANDING_CXX` as a flag (option, no value), but
`_add_target_variant_c_compile_flags` and
`_add_target_variant_c_compile_link_flags` were parsing it as a
single-valued argument and the callers forwarded it as
`NO_FREESTANDING_CXX "${SWIFTLIB_SINGLE_NO_FREESTANDING_CXX}"`
the literal string `TRUE`/`FALSE` as the argument value.

Move the parameter to each helper's options list and use `translate_flag`
at the call sites so the keyword is either present or absent, matching
the shape everywhere else.

author: Doug Gregor
message:
[CMake] Cap embedded-stdlib Swift compilations with a ninja job pool

Embedded stdlib cross-compilation runs one swiftc per (module x triple),
and each of those holds ~2 GB resident during IRGen. With ~28 embedded
triples plus per-triple shim/overlay libraries, an unrestricted -j36
build lets ninja schedule dozens of these concurrently. On Linux CI that
burst lines up with the finish of libsourcekitdInProc.so's LTO link and
OOMs the runner -- three separate Linux PR jobs died at exactly the same
ninja step (1644/2955).

Route every embedded-SDK Swift compile through a dedicated CMake job pool
sized at ceil(cores / 4) by default. Non-embedded compilations (host
stdlib, SDK overlays) still run in the default pool at full parallelism.
SWIFT_PARALLEL_EMBEDDED_COMPILE_JOBS overrides the depth when needed.

Also thread a JOB_POOL argument through the shared
add_custom_command_target helper so _compile_swift_files can attach
the pool to each swiftc invocation it emits.

author: Saleem Abdulrasool
message:
AST: fold the metatype-extension check into a DeclContext helper

The `dyn_cast<ExtensionDecl>(dc)` / `->isMetatypeExtension()` dance was
repeated across name lookup, the constraint solver, and existential
member handling.  Introduce `DeclContext::isMetatypeExtension()` — which
returns false for any context that is not a metatype extension — and
adopt it at the sites that only need the predicate.

While here, form the protocol metatype `Self` in `getSelfInterfaceType`
from an explicit `ProtocolDecl` (`getDeclaredExistentialType()`) rather
than reconstructing `any P` from a bare nominal, so the existential is
built only where we actually have a protocol rather than relying on a
distant `isa<ProtocolDecl>` guard.

author: Allan Shortlidge
message:
AST: Move availability restriction queries onto AvailabilityContext.

Expose availability restriction queries as methods on `AvailabilityContext`
instead of as free functions that take a context as an input, making query
callsites less verbose throughout the compiler.

NFC.

author: Evan Wilde
message:
Merge pull request "ELF Metadata: Retain the metadata table" (#90592)

Retain the metadata bounds table and mark section bounds as weak symbols so that if they are stripped in the event that the section is empty and you're working with `lld` the bounds will be `NULL` instead of causing a failed link.

author: Lucas Ly Ba
message:
[SILOptimizer] Simplify Int1 comparisons with boolean constants

`x == false` and `!x` are equivalent on Bool but lowered to different
builtins (cmp_eq_Int1 vs xor_Int1), so only `!x` optimized well. Fold
comparisons of a Builtin.Int1 with a boolean constant so both forms
produce the same SIL.

Fixes #70655

author: Erik Eckstein

Those passes are replaced by DeadObjectElimination

author: Erik Eckstein
message:
MandatoryPerformanceOptimizations: use `removeDeadStackAllocations` from the new DeadObjectElimination pass

Instead of `eliminateDeadAllocations` from the old PredictableDeadAllocationElimination pass

author: Erik Eckstein
message:
Optimizer: rewrite the DeadObjectElimination pass

DeadObjectElimination  eliminates dead `alloc_stack` and `alloc_ref` allocations.

**Stack allocations**

A stack allocation is dead if its stored values never escape and all loads can be forwarded directly to the stored values:

```
  %s = alloc_stack $Foo
  store %v to [init] %s : $*Foo
  %l = load [take] %s : $*Foo
  dealloc_stack %s : $*Foo
```
->
```
  // %l is replaced with %v
```

This also handles field-level stores and loads. Destroy instructions for field values whose containing object is destroyed replace the original aggregate destroy:

```
  %s = alloc_stack $Foo
  %fa = struct_element_addr %s : $*Foo, #Foo.x
  store %x to [init] %fa : $*Int
  destroy_addr %s : $*Foo
  dealloc_stack %s : $*Foo
```
->
```
  destroy_value %x : $Int
```

**Heap allocations**

A heap allocation is dead if it never escapes and its destructor has no observable side effects. Stored field values are destroyed in place of the object destructor:

```
  %obj = alloc_ref $Foo
  %addr = ref_element_addr %obj : $Foo, #Foo.x
  store %x to [init] %addr : $*Int
  destroy_value %obj : $Foo
```
->
```
  destroy_value %x : $Int
```

The pass replaces the old DeadObjectElimination and SILMem2Reg. It combines the advantages of both passes:

* The old DeadObjectElimination can only remove an `alloc_stack` or `alloc_ref` if there are no loads from such allocations.

* SILMem2Reg can handle loads, but can only remove `alloc_stack`s and not `alloc_ref`s.

The new pass can handle loads from both, `alloc_stack` and `alloc_ref`.
This means, for example, it can do array element propagation (an array buffer is an `alloc_ref) and, if there are no other uses of the array,
completely eliminate the array.

Also, the new pass has other improvements compared to the old passes. E.g. it can deal with objects where the individual fields are initialized with separate stores.

This change also adds the pass variant MandatoryDeadObjectElimination. It replaces the old PredictableDeadAllocationElimination pass.

author: Erik Eckstein
message:
SIL: fix a compile time problem in SILInstructionResultArray

When doing invariant checks, avoid quadratic complexity for multi-value instructions with many results.
Just check the first 8 results, which is good enough for the common case.

author: Erik Eckstein
message:
Optimizer: move `insertMarkDependencies` from RedundantLoadElimination.swift to OptUtils.swift

So that it can be used from other passes, too.
